content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN. Chef constructs the range-array AL,RA_{L,R}AL,R‚Äč as follows:



First, Chef considers the subarray AL,AL+1,...,ARA_L, A_{L + 1}, . . . , A_{R}AL‚Äč,AL+1‚Äč,...,AR‚Äč.




Then Chef removes the integers that do not belong to the range [L,R][L, R][L,R] from the subarray to finally create the range-array AL,RA_{L, R}AL,R‚Äč.



For example, consider the array A=[1,3,2,4]A = [1, 3, 2, 4]A=[1,3,2,4], then the range-arrays are: A1,1=[1],A1,2=[1],A1,3=[1,3,2],A1,4=[1,3,2,4],A_{1, 1} = [1], A_{1, 2} = [1], A_{1, 3} = [1, 3, 2], A_{1, 4} = [1, 3, 2, 4],A1,1‚Äč=[1],A1,2‚Äč=[1],A1,3‚Äč=[1,3,2],A1,4‚Äč=[1,3,2,4], A2,2=[‚ÄÖ‚Ää]A_{2, 2} = [\;]A2,2‚Äč=[], A2,3=[3,2],A_{2, 3} = [3, 2],A2,3‚Äč=[3,2], A2,4=[3,2,4]A_{2, 4} = [3, 2, 4]A2,4‚Äč=[3,2,4] and so on.

Chef is given a permutation A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2,\dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč of length NNN. A pair of integers (L,R)‚ÄÖ‚Ää(1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N)(L, R) \;(1 \leq L \leq R \leq N)(L,R)(1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N) is called good if all the elements in the range array AL,RA_{L, R}AL,R‚Äč are sorted in increasing order. In case, a range array is an empty list, it is considered to be sorted. Chef tries to find the number of good pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R), but fails. Can you help him?


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integer A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2,\dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the number of good pairs.
constraints: A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2,\dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč is a permutation.
Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105.
Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200200200.
Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 250025002500.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The range arrays are A1,1=[‚ÄÖ‚Ää],A2,2=[‚ÄÖ‚Ää],A1,2=[2,1]A_{1, 1} = [\;], A_{2, 2} = [\;], A_{1, 2} = [2, 1]A1,1‚Äč=[],A2,2‚Äč=[],A1,2‚Äč=[2,1]. The first two of them are empty lists and hence sorted.
Test case 222: Elements in all the six range arrays are sorted in increasing order.
